To determine the speed of the train in kilometers per hour (km/h), we begin by analyzing the given data:

- **Length of the train**: 125.01 meters  
- **Time to cross a pole**: 5 seconds  

---

### Step 1: Calculate Speed in Meters per Second (m/s)

When a train crosses a pole, the distance it travels is equal to its own length. So, the speed in meters per second is:

$$
\text{Speed} = \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Time}} = \frac{125.01}{5} = 25.002 \text{ m/s}
$$

---

### Step 2: Convert Meters per Second to Kilometers per Hour

To convert from m/s to km/h, we use the conversion factor:

$$
1 \text{ m/s} = 3.6 \text{ km/h}
$$

So,

$$
25.002 \text{ m/s} \times 3.6 = 90.0072 \text{ km/h}
$$

---

### Final Answer

$$
\boxed{90.0072}
$$
